edges.description: Loss of CFAP418 function at the ciliary base impairs the photoreceptor connecting cilium through which all outer-segment cargo is trafficked.
edges.source: CFAP418 Loss of Function at the Photoreceptor Ciliary Base

edges.description: At the severe (typically null) end of the allelic series, the same proximal ciliary-base loss of function extends beyond the photoreceptor to systemic ciliated tissues, producing the Bardet-Biedl (BBS21) multisystem phenotype.
edges.source: CFAP418 Loss of Function at the Photoreceptor Ciliary Base

edges.description: Connecting-cilium dysfunction drives progressive death of rod and cone photoreceptors.
Rod and cone photoreceptor loss manifests clinically as cone-rod dystrophy or retinitis pigmentosa.
Progressive photoreceptor loss reduces visual acuity and field.

nodes.description: CFAP418/C8orf37 is a cilium-associated protein enriched at the base of the photoreceptor connecting cilium, where it interacts with the ciliary protein FAM161A. Biallelic loss-of-function or missense variants disrupt this ciliary-base function, compromising the gating and trafficking machinery on which the highly cilium-dependent photoreceptor relies.
nodes.id: CFAP418 Loss of Function at the Photoreceptor Ciliary Base

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8orf37, encoding a ciliary protein, are associated with autosomal-recessive retinal dystrophies with early macular involvement.
evidence.snippet: Immunohistochemical studies revealed C8orf37 localization at the base of the primary cilium of human retinal pigment epithelium cells and at the base of connecting cilia of mouse photoreceptors.

evidence.reference_title: Interactions between C8orf37 and FAM161A, Two Ciliary Proteins Essential for Photoreceptor Survival.
evidence.snippet: FAM161A, a microtubule-binding protein localized at the photoreceptor cilium required for photoreceptor survival, was identified as one of the preys.

nodes.description: Cone-rod dystrophy (CORD16) is one of the defining non-syndromic retinal presentations of CFAP418-related disease, with early macular involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8orf37, encoding a ciliary protein, are associated with autosomal-recessive retinal dystrophies with early macular involvement.
evidence.snippet: Clinical assessment revealed CRD in four individuals and RP with early macular involvement in two individuals.

nodes.description: At the severe (typically null) end of the CFAP418 allelic series, ciliary dysfunction extends beyond the retina to the systemic branches of the ciliopathy spectrum, producing the Bardet-Biedl syndrome phenotype (obesity, postaxial polydactyly, renal anomalies, cognitive impairment) - BBS21.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8ORF37 cause Bardet Biedl syndrome (BBS21).
evidence.snippet: The proband was overweight (body mass index 29.1) with a slowly progressive rod-cone dystrophy, a mild learning difficulty, high myopia, three limb post-axial polydactyly, horseshoe kidney, abnormally positioned uterus and elevated liver enzymes.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8ORF37 cause Bardet Biedl syndrome (BBS21).
evidence.snippet: Knockdown of c8orf37 resulted in impaired visual behavior and BBS-related phenotypes, specifically, defects in the formation of Kupffer's vesicle and delays in retrograde transport.

nodes.description: Obesity is a cardinal Bardet-Biedl syndrome feature present at the syndromic end of the CFAP418 spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8ORF37 cause Bardet Biedl syndrome (BBS21).
evidence.snippet: The proband was overweight (body mass index 29.1)

nodes.description: The photoreceptor outer segment is a specialized sensory cilium connected to the cell body by the connecting cilium, through which all phototransduction cargo passes. CFAP418-related ciliary-base dysfunction impairs outer-segment morphogenesis and maintenance, the retinal arm shared across the CFAP418 allelic series.

evidence.reference_title: Interactions between C8orf37 and FAM161A, Two Ciliary Proteins Essential for Photoreceptor Survival.
evidence.snippet: C8orf37 was enriched and was co-localized with FAM161A at the ciliary base of photoreceptors.

nodes.description: Progressive degeneration of rod and cone photoreceptors is the shared final common pathway of CFAP418-related disease, manifesting as cone-rod dystrophy, retinitis pigmentosa with early macular involvement, or the retinal component of Bardet-Biedl syndrome depending on the allele.

evidence.reference_title: Interactions between C8orf37 and FAM161A, Two Ciliary Proteins Essential for Photoreceptor Survival.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in C8orf37 cause Bardet-Biedl syndrome (BBS), retinitis pigmentosa (RP), and cone-rod dystrophy (CRD), all manifest in photoreceptor degeneration.

nodes.description: MC4R agonist (IMCIVREE) approved for the treatment of obesity and hyperphagia in Bardet-Biedl syndrome. It is relevant to the syndromic BBS21 arm of the CFAP418 allelic series, in which extra-retinal ciliary pleiotropy produces early-onset obesity; setmelanotide pharmacologically mitigates the hyperphagia and obesity rather than correcting the underlying ciliary defect.

evidence.reference_title: Bardet-Biedl syndrome: A clinical overview focusing on diagnosis, outcomes and best-practice management.
evidence.snippet: setmelanotide is a new pharmacotherapy approved for treatment of obesity in BBS.

nodes.description: Reduced visual acuity from progressive photoreceptor degeneration across the CFAP418 spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8ORF37 cause Bardet Biedl syndrome (BBS21).
evidence.snippet: The proband was overweight (body mass index 29.1) with a slowly progressive rod-cone dystrophy, a mild learning difficulty, high myopia

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in C8ORF37 cause Bardet Biedl syndrome (BBS21).
evidence.snippet: Knockdown of c8orf37 resulted in impaired visual behavior and BBS-related phenotypes
